outlook.headline: Production test demand is broadening, but still early-stage
outlook.summary: Production test supply looks set to improve modestly over the next 1-2 quarters as demand pull broadens from early commercialization into actual production ramps. Teradyne is seeing first multi-system production test orders, FormFactor is benefiting from TRITON ramp and advanced-packaging final-test roles, and Cohu’s near-80% utilization suggests capacity remains tight. The key watch is whether this current, only slightly above-average mention trend translates into sustained order conversion rather than a short-lived burst.

drivers.label: Commercialization orders are starting to convert
drivers.detail: Teradyne’s first multi-system production test orders point to real demand pull rather than just interest.

drivers.label: Ramp in advanced packaging and final-test roles
drivers.detail: FormFactor’s TRITON ramp and expanded final-test exposure indicate broader production-test demand.

drivers.label: Capacity remains relatively tight
drivers.detail: Cohu’s near-80% utilization signals healthy demand and limited spare capacity in the near term.

drivers.label: Mention trend is only modestly positive
drivers.detail: The topic mention trend is flat-to-slightly up (latest 0.1x, +2% vs trailing average), implying improvement is real but not yet broad-based.

risks: Demand pull could remain concentrated in a few early commercialization wins rather than broaden sector-wide.
TRITON or advanced-packaging ramps could slip, delaying the expected uplift in production-test demand.
Cohu utilization could ease if customers push out volumes or if supply tightness normalizes faster than expected.
